evidence.hypothesis: TWAP repeatedly constructs account/stock cache keys and reads order status during position and order-state paths; narrow key reuse and duplicate-lookup removal may reduce deterministic overhead without changing payload shape.
evidence.expected_effect: Reduce repeated string construction and cache lookup overhead on TWAP status paths.

source_evidence.notes: Move away from position-push structural rewrites; only allow local lookup/key construction cleanup.

evidence.hypothesis: TWAP order config access builds stock/account keys and decodes params on hot paths; a narrow patch can avoid repeated temporary work while preserving config semantics.
evidence.expected_effect: Reduce local config lookup overhead without changing selected/sellRatio/params behavior.

source_evidence.notes: Focus on deterministic repeated lookup work; do not rewrite aggregation or push fanout.

insight.hypothesis: Subscription matching repeatedly parses or scans request search/account filters; precomputing request-local filter terms may reduce fanout scan overhead without changing which subscribers receive messages.
insight.expected_effect: Reduce subscription filter overhead while preserving userId/searchStockCode semantics.

source_evidence.notes: Allowed only if the patch keeps the same subscriber inclusion/exclusion behavior.

insight.hypothesis: Redis update ingest may do parse or trigger work before rejecting irrelevant or malformed events; a guard-first patch can reduce wasted work without dropping valid events.
insight.expected_effect: Reduce ingest overhead for irrelevant updates while preserving event delivery.

source_evidence.notes: High risk: correctness must prove no lost valid update before timing matters.
